productVision: Create an AI-native real property operating system that becomes the trusted system of action and record for homes, portfolios, service work, and community operations. Hestami should reduce effort, uncertainty, and fragmentation by turning property issues, decisions, and workflows into coordinated, auditable execution across owners, providers, and associations.
productDescription: Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform is a multi-sided property operations platform that unifies three connected pillars: an owner-facing assistant for homeowners, small landlords, and investors; a service operations workspace for providers and field teams; and an association operations product for community managers, boards, and management companies. Across these pillars, the platform centers on a canonical property record, AI-assisted request intake, end-to-end case orchestration, provider discovery and coordination, scheduling and field execution, approvals and payments, communications, documents and evidence, governance workflows, self-service portals, recurring maintenance, analytics, and a durable decision ledger. The product is designed so every participant works from the same operational context, with clearer accountability, better trust signals, and continuity from intake through completion and long-term recordkeeping.
summary: Hestami is positioned as a real property operating system that connects fragmented work, decisions, and records across the lifecycle of maintaining and operating homes, portfolios, and communities. Its core users span individual homeowners, small landlords, investors, residents, service providers, field technicians, community association managers, board members, management companies, and Hestami staff. The product settles into three linked pillars: owner-side property assistance, provider-side service execution, and community association operations. Under those pillars, the strongest throughline is a shared operational foundation built on property context, structured intake, case and workflow orchestration, communications, documents, approvals, payments, and auditable decision history. The homeowner and portfolio experience focuses on turning vague property needs into trusted, trackable outcomes, while the provider experience focuses on estimating, scheduling, dispatch, invoicing, and mobile field context. The association pillar extends the same operational model into community work management, owner interactions, board governance, and portfolio oversight across multiple communities. The phased shape is clear: establish the common operating foundation and initial user wedge first, then deepen service-provider workflows, and later expand into fuller community association capability on top of the same shared system of record.

steps.expectedOutcome: The property has a canonical record that can support future maintenance and decisions

steps.action: Invites a spouse, co-owner, or delegated contact and defines who can approve or coordinate work
steps.expectedOutcome: Access and authority are clear before any service actions begin

steps.action: Submits a maintenance or repair request in free-form language with photos and urgency details

steps.action: Interprets the request, categorizes the problem, suggests likely trades, and highlights any missing information or possible permit or HOA constraints

phasingStrategy.description: Deliver the homeowner and small-portfolio value wedge: property setup, request intake, service-call case management, provider discovery and matching, and transparent coordination history.
phasingStrategy.rationale: This is the clearest stated pain point and the most specific Hestami-defined problem: helping property owners reduce effort and risk in maintenance and repair coordination.

phasingStrategy.description: Expand into contractor-side operational software so service providers can run scheduling, dispatch, estimating, execution, and billing workflows inside the same ecosystem.
phasingStrategy.rationale: Once owner-side demand capture and matching exist, provider-side operations deepen marketplace value, improve service quality, and create two-sided platform defensibility.

phasingStrategy.description: Add community association management capabilities for managers, boards, owners, and residents, including portal, governance, communication, and association operations workflows.
phasingStrategy.rationale: CAM is strategically aligned but broader and operationally denser. It likely benefits from lessons and shared data models established in earlier owner and provider workflows.

successMetrics: Percentage of homeowner service calls that progress from intake to matched provider recommendation
Median time from homeowner request submission to first qualified provider match
Percentage of service calls completed with a full recorded decision and outcome history
Reduction in homeowner-reported effort to coordinate maintenance or repair work
Percentage of provider proposals accepted without requiring offline clarification due to inconsistent scope
Number of properties managed per landlord or investor account with active service history captured in platform
For later phases, percentage of contractor jobs managed end to end inside the platform
For later phases, percentage of community requests or action items completed through the platform rather than off-platform channels

businessDomainProposals.name: Property Record and Context
businessDomainProposals.description: Canonical property profiles including address, structure details, units, systems, media, documents, lifecycle history, and contextual facts needed to operate on a property confidently.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly required by property registration, property setup, and durable record objectives in UJ-1, UJ-4, and the core product vision of becoming the system of record for real property. This domain anchors all three pillars because service work, governance, and portfolio reporting all depend on accurate property context.

businessDomainProposals.name: Ownership, Roles, and Delegated Authority
businessDomainProposals.description: Modeling of owners, co-owners, residents, tenants, board members, managers, delegated contacts, and authority relationships that determine who can view, request, approve, or act on behalf of a property or association.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the requirement for ownership roles, delegated authority, and relationships. It is also necessary for role-based portals, community governance, portfolio operations, and controlled service coordination across homeowners, tenants, managers, boards, and Hestami staff.

businessDomainProposals.name: Portfolio and Multi-Property Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Grouping, monitoring, and operating multiple homes, units, or associations under one owner, operator, or management company view with shared controls and reporting.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly driven by UJ-4 and the needs of small landlords, investors, and management companies. The product description also states support for small portfolio owners and community management organizations, making portfolio operations a first-class domain rather than a reporting afterthought.

businessDomainProposals.name: Request Intake and Intent Capture
businessDomainProposals.description: Free-form and structured intake of maintenance, repair, improvement, association, and resident requests, including AI-assisted interpretation, categorization, and enrichment.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly required by free-form owner intent and request intake, homeowner service call creation, and resident self-service workflows. It is foundational because nearly every operational journey begins with a request, issue, or action item entering the system.
workflowPreview: Route request to the correct workflow

businessDomainProposals.name: Service Case and Work Lifecycle
businessDomainProposals.description: End-to-end case management from intake through triage, scheduling, changes, completion, closure, and post-resolution recordkeeping for homeowner, portfolio, and community work.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the durable service call lifecycle and UJ-2 and UJ-5. This is central to the platform vision of reducing fragmentation and maintaining a transparent operational history across property-related work.

businessDomainProposals.name: Service Provider Discovery, Matching, and Trust
businessDomainProposals.description: Identification, qualification, comparison, and selection of service providers using trade fit, availability, geography, licensing, insurance, reputation, and prior performance signals.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly implied by UJ-3 and explicitly required by service provider discovery and matching facilitated by AI agents or Hestami staff. It addresses homeowner pain around trust, legitimacy checks, and inconsistent vendor selection while also supporting CAM vendor coordination.

businessDomainProposals.name: External Vendor Coordination
businessDomainProposals.description: Operational collaboration with vendors whether or not they are full platform users, including outreach, scheduling, status updates, document exchange, and evidence capture.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required because the homeowner pillar must support external vendor coordination without requiring vendor onboarding. This is essential for real-world service execution where many contractors will initially interact through lightweight channels rather than adopt the full provider product.

businessDomainProposals.name: Scheduling, Dispatch, and Field Execution
businessDomainProposals.description: Planning visits, assigning technicians, managing availability, routing work, tracking on-site execution, and handling reschedules across contractor and association operations.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the field service management pillar and UJ-6. It is also relevant to Hestami staff coordination in Phase 1 and association work orders in Phase 3, making it a cross-pillar execution domain.

businessDomainProposals.name: Estimates, Proposals, and Approvals
businessDomainProposals.description: Creation, comparison, review, negotiation, and approval of scopes, estimates, bids, change orders, and related decision checkpoints for property and association work.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly driven by UJ-7 and homeowner pain around inconsistent estimates and comparison difficulty. It also intersects with board approvals, owner approvals, and improvement work where formal review and acceptance are required.

businessDomainProposals.name: Billing, Invoicing, and Payments
businessDomainProposals.description: Financial workflows tied to completed work, including invoicing, payment requests, receipts, and status tracking for homeowners, providers, and associations.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Invoicing is explicitly called out in the service-provider pillar, and payment completion is a standard end-state for property work. While the source material emphasizes operations more than accounting depth, this domain is necessary to complete the contractor job lifecycle and to support association vendor payments and owner-facing financial transparency.

businessDomainProposals.name: Communications and Collaboration
businessDomainProposals.description: Unified messaging, notifications, updates, and threaded collaboration among owners, residents, vendors, managers, board members, and internal staff across all operational workflows.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The product vision explicitly targets fragmentation in communications, and multiple personas cite poor visibility and manual back-and-forth as pain points. This domain supports case updates, resident portals, board coordination, vendor communication, and transparent stakeholder alignment.

businessDomainProposals.name: Documents, Evidence, and Records Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Storage, organization, retrieval, and operational use of permits, approvals, correspondence, inspection artifacts, meeting materials, reserve studies, and other supporting records.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the homeowner pillar and CAM pillar, including permits, approvals, correspondence, documents, and reserve studies. It also underpins the product vision of being the durable system of record and supports auditability across decisions and execution.
workflowPreview: Associate records to cases or communities

businessDomainProposals.name: Decision Ledger and Audit Trail
businessDomainProposals.description: A transparent ledger of material decisions, rationale, supporting evidence, actor identity, and timeline history across property, service, and governance workflows.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: This is explicitly required in the homeowner pillar and strongly aligned with the product vision of reducing uncertainty and preserving trust. It is especially important for owner-provider decisions, board governance, staff-assisted workflows, and cross-party disputes about what was decided and why.

businessDomainProposals.name: Permits, Compliance, and Property Constraints
businessDomainProposals.description: Tracking of HOA constraints, architectural approvals, permits, policy requirements, violations, and other rules that affect whether and how work may proceed.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the homeowner pillar's need to track HOA constraints and approvals even when the HOA is not a platform participant, and by the CAM requirement to cover violations and ARC requests. This domain reduces risk in repair and improvement decisions and connects owner workflows with association governance.

businessDomainProposals.name: Association Operations and Work Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Operational management of association tasks, common-area work, owner interactions, vendor work orders, violations, and ongoing community action items.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly driven by UJ-8 and the CAM requirement covering association and property operations including work orders and violations. This is the operating core for community managers and management companies, analogous to service case management on the homeowner side.
workflowPreview: Track issue resolution across the community

businessDomainProposals.name: Governance, Board Process, and Meetings
businessDomainProposals.description: Support for board reviews, approvals, agenda and meeting materials, governance actions, voting, policy decisions, and secure board visibility into community operations.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Directly required by UJ-9 and the CAM pillar's governance responsibilities. Board members need structured decision support and secure records, and managers need a workflow that turns governance from fragmented email chains into trackable actions.

businessDomainProposals.name: Role-Based Portals and Self-Service Experiences
businessDomainProposals.description: Tailored portal experiences for homeowners, residents, tenants, vendors, managers, and board members to complete common tasks, view relevant records, and receive updates.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the CAM pillar and UJ-10, and also implied by homeowner and provider needs for lower-friction interactions. This domain operationalizes access control, communications, and request handling into usable experiences for each stakeholder type.

businessDomainProposals.name: Recurring Service and Preventive Maintenance
businessDomainProposals.description: Planning and execution of recurring jobs, seasonal maintenance, inspections, and preventive care for homes, rental portfolios, and communities.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Recurring service operations are explicitly required in the service-provider pillar and naturally extend the property's lifecycle record objective. This domain supports homeowners wanting continuity, landlords managing repeat needs, and associations maintaining shared assets over time.

businessDomainProposals.name: Trade-Specific Service Operations
businessDomainProposals.description: Support for targeted trade industries with domain-specific intake, job structures, scopes, artifacts, and execution patterns rather than a generic business-management model.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required because the service-provider pillar must support targeted trade industries rather than a generic use case. This domain allows differentiated workflows for trades such as HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, landscaping, restoration, or inspections where job data and operational patterns differ materially.

businessDomainProposals.name: Operational Analytics and Performance Visibility
businessDomainProposals.description: Dashboards, KPIs, and reporting across cases, vendors, portfolios, communities, responsiveness, throughput, and service outcomes for owners, providers, managers, and internal admins.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Portfolio-level visibility is explicitly important for investors, management companies, and managers, and the product vision emphasizes lower friction and higher trust through shared operational picture. Analytics is a standard domain needed to convert unified workflow data into actionable oversight.
workflowPreview: Compare outcomes across properties or communities

businessDomainProposals.name: Hestami Internal Operations and Administration
businessDomainProposals.description: Internal tooling for Hestami staff to coordinate service calls, administer users and workflows, supervise AI-human handoffs, and maintain quality and trust across the platform.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Explicitly required by the homeowner pillar's support for human Hestami staff service-call execution and outcome recording, and directly mapped to P-9 and UJ-3 and UJ-5. This domain is necessary because Hestami itself is an operational participant in the product model, not just a software vendor.

entityProposals.name: Property
entityProposals.description: Canonical record for a physical property managed or referenced by the platform.

entityProposals.name: Address
entityProposals.description: Normalized address and geolocation details for a property or related site.

entityProposals.name: Property Structure
entityProposals.description: Structural characteristics of a property such as building type, size, rooms, and construction facts.

entityProposals.name: Property Unit
entityProposals.description: A rentable, occupiable, or separately managed unit within a property or community.

entityProposals.name: Property System
entityProposals.description: Installed system or asset within a property such as HVAC, roof, plumbing, electrical, or appliance groups.

entityProposals.name: Property Context Fact
entityProposals.description: Important contextual fact about a property that influences operations, decisions, or service execution.

entityProposals.name: Property Media
entityProposals.description: Photo, video, floorplan image, or other visual media associated with a property record.

entityProposals.name: Property Lifecycle Event
entityProposals.description: Historical event in a property's timeline such as acquisition, renovation, inspection, or occupancy change.

entityProposals.name: Party
entityProposals.description: A person or organization participating in ownership, occupancy, management, service, or governance workflows.

entityProposals.name: User Account
entityProposals.description: Platform login and identity record for a human user linked to a party.

entityProposals.name: Contact Method
entityProposals.description: Phone, email, mailing address, or other channel for reaching a party.

entityProposals.name: Party Role Assignment
entityProposals.description: Assignment of an owner, tenant, manager, board, vendor, or internal role to a party within a property or community context.

entityProposals.name: Property Authority Grant
entityProposals.description: Delegated authority describing who may request, approve, coordinate, or view information for a property or case.

entityProposals.name: Occupancy
entityProposals.description: Occupancy relationship linking a resident or tenant party to a property unit over time.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio
entityProposals.description: A grouping of properties or communities managed together under one owner, investor, or operator view.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio Asset
entityProposals.description: Junction record linking a portfolio to a managed property or community.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio Control Policy
entityProposals.description: Shared operating or approval policy applied across a portfolio of properties or communities.

entityProposals.name: Request
entityProposals.description: Raw or structured intake record representing a reported issue, need, question, or work intent.

entityProposals.name: Request Intent
entityProposals.description: Structured interpretation of what the requester needs, including category, urgency, probable trade, and recommended next step.

entityProposals.name: Intake Artifact
entityProposals.description: Supplemental intake content such as questionnaire answers, access notes, symptom details, or AI-extracted facts.

entityProposals.name: AI Intake Run
entityProposals.description: Record of an AI-assisted intake interpretation pass, including model outputs, confidence, and handoff status.

entityProposals.name: Service Case
entityProposals.description: Primary operational case that manages work from triage through closure.

entityProposals.name: Case Stage Event
entityProposals.description: Timeline event capturing state transitions, triage outcomes, hold reasons, reopen events, or closure milestones for a case.

entityProposals.name: Work Order
entityProposals.description: Executable work package derived from a case for scheduling, dispatch, and completion tracking.

entityProposals.name: Case Participant
entityProposals.description: Junction entity linking parties to a case with a functional role such as owner, tenant, manager, vendor, or observer.

entityProposals.name: Case Resolution
entityProposals.description: Structured outcome record describing final diagnosis, work performed, result, and closure classification for a case.

entityProposals.name: Service Provider Profile
entityProposals.description: Discoverable provider organization profile including trade coverage, service areas, trust signals, and operating preferences.

entityProposals.name: Provider Trade Capability
entityProposals.description: Specific trade or specialty a provider is qualified to perform.

entityProposals.name: Provider Credential
entityProposals.description: License, insurance, bond, certification, or compliance credential for a provider.

entityProposals.name: Provider Service Area
entityProposals.description: Geographic area in which a provider offers services.

entityProposals.name: Provider Performance Record
entityProposals.description: Historical quality, timeliness, response, and outcome metrics tied to completed work by a provider.

entityProposals.name: Provider Match
entityProposals.description: Ranked matching result between a case or work need and candidate providers.

entityProposals.name: Vendor Outreach
entityProposals.description: Outbound coordination attempt or invitation sent to a vendor regarding a case, estimate, or job opportunity.

entityProposals.name: Vendor External Access
entityProposals.description: Limited-access tokenized access grant for a vendor who is not a full platform user.

entityProposals.name: Vendor Status Update
entityProposals.description: Status, note, attachment, or requested action submitted by a vendor during coordination.

entityProposals.name: Job Visit
entityProposals.description: Scheduled on-site or remote appointment instance for executing work under a work order.

entityProposals.name: Dispatch Assignment
entityProposals.description: Assignment of a technician, crew, or provider resource to a scheduled job visit.

entityProposals.name: Availability Slot
entityProposals.description: Represented availability window for a provider, technician, or crew used in scheduling.

entityProposals.name: Field Update
entityProposals.description: Mobile or operational update from the field describing arrival, delay, work progress, findings, or completion.

entityProposals.name: Estimate
entityProposals.description: Vendor or internal estimate or proposal for a defined scope of work.

entityProposals.name: Estimate Line Item
entityProposals.description: Line-level labor, material, allowance, or fee component within an estimate.

entityProposals.name: Change Order
entityProposals.description: Post-approval change to scope, price, schedule, or assumptions requiring review and acceptance.

entityProposals.name: Approval Decision
entityProposals.description: Formal approval, rejection, or revision decision on an estimate, change order, or gated action.

entityProposals.name: Invoice
entityProposals.description: Bill issued for completed or partially completed work tied to a case or work order.

entityProposals.name: Invoice Line Item
entityProposals.description: Charge component on an invoice such as labor, materials, fees, or taxes.

entityProposals.name: Payment
entityProposals.description: Recorded payment attempt or completed payment against an invoice or billing request.

entityProposals.name: Receipt
entityProposals.description: Receipt or settlement confirmation generated after successful payment capture or recording.

entityProposals.name: Conversation Thread
entityProposals.description: Unified thread for messages, comments, and operational collaboration around a shared topic or workflow item.

entityProposals.name: Message
entityProposals.description: Individual communication entry within a conversation thread, including human or system-originated content.

entityProposals.name: Conversation Participant
entityProposals.description: Junction entity linking parties to a conversation thread with participation and visibility settings.

entityProposals.name: Notification
entityProposals.description: Routed alert, reminder, or status notification sent to a user or external contact.

entityProposals.name: Notification Endpoint
entityProposals.description: Delivery endpoint and preference configuration for a user's notifications.

entityProposals.name: File Asset
entityProposals.description: Stored digital file such as document, image, video, or scanned artifact used across workflows.

entityProposals.name: Document Record
entityProposals.description: Metadata wrapper for an operational document, permit, approval, correspondence set, or evidence bundle.

entityProposals.name: Document Link
entityProposals.description: Junction linking a document or file asset to another business object such as a property, case, estimate, or meeting.

entityProposals.name: Document Version
entityProposals.description: Versioned file and metadata history for a document record.

entityProposals.name: Decision Record
entityProposals.description: Authoritative ledger entry for a material decision including actor, rationale, timing, and outcome.

entityProposals.name: Decision Participant
entityProposals.description: Junction linking parties to a decision with roles such as decider, recommender, reviewer, or informed party.

entityProposals.name: Decision Evidence Link
entityProposals.description: Link between a decision and the supporting documents, messages, cases, or artifacts used as evidence.

entityProposals.name: Compliance Rule
entityProposals.description: Rule, permit requirement, policy constraint, HOA condition, or legal requirement that may affect work.

entityProposals.name: Compliance Evaluation
entityProposals.description: Evaluation result determining whether a request, case, or work order is blocked, conditional, or clear to proceed.

entityProposals.name: Permit Application
entityProposals.description: Permit or external approval application associated with planned or ongoing work.

entityProposals.name: Violation Record
entityProposals.description: Recorded property or association violation, including notices, status, and remediation path.

entityProposals.name: Community Association
entityProposals.description: Association or HOA entity representing a managed community with operational and governance context.

entityProposals.name: Association Asset
entityProposals.description: Common-area asset, facility, or managed component belonging to a community association.

entityProposals.name: Association Task
entityProposals.description: Operational work item for community management such as owner interaction, common-area issue, follow-up, or remediation task.

entityProposals.name: Board Meeting
entityProposals.description: Scheduled board meeting with agenda, materials, attendance, and archived governance outputs.

entityProposals.name: Meeting Agenda Item
entityProposals.description: Individual agenda topic, packet item, or board review subject for a meeting.

entityProposals.name: Vote Record
entityProposals.description: Recorded board vote outcome and member-level votes for a governance action.

entityProposals.name: Vote Response
entityProposals.description: Individual board member vote cast on a vote record.

entityProposals.name: Portal Task
entityProposals.description: Role-targeted self-service task or action surfaced in a portal experience.

entityProposals.name: Portal View Config
entityProposals.description: Configuration defining what records, actions, and widgets are visible to a role in a portal context.

entityProposals.name: Asset Maintenance Plan
entityProposals.description: Preventive or recurring service plan for a property system or association asset.

entityProposals.name: Recurring Service Occurrence
entityProposals.description: Generated scheduled occurrence from a maintenance plan that may become a case, work order, or visit.

entityProposals.name: Trade Schema
entityProposals.description: Trade-specific schema defining intake fields, scope structures, required artifacts, and execution patterns for a specialty.

entityProposals.name: Trade Artifact Requirement
entityProposals.description: Artifact, form, measurement, or evidence requirement associated with a trade schema.

entityProposals.name: KPI Definition
entityProposals.description: Definition of a tracked metric, including formula, grain, and intended reporting context.

entityProposals.name: KPI Observation
entityProposals.description: Computed KPI value for a given period and scope such as property, provider, portfolio, or community.

entityProposals.name: Exception Alert
entityProposals.description: Analytics-driven exception or anomaly flag indicating an operational issue needing attention.

entityProposals.name: Internal Task
entityProposals.description: Internal Hestami operational task for supervision, follow-up, quality control, or assisted coordination.

entityProposals.name: Human AI Handoff
entityProposals.description: Recorded handoff between AI and human operators capturing trigger, reason, ownership, and outcome.

entityProposals.name: Workflow Config
entityProposals.description: Configurable workflow rules, routing, and feature settings used by internal administrators.

entityProposals.name: Audit Event
entityProposals.description: Low-level immutable audit event recording user or system actions on sensitive records and configurations.

workflowProposals.name: Canonical property record creation and enrichment
workflowProposals.description: Creates and maintains a complete property profile used by all downstream workflows.
steps: Ingest address and structure details from manual entry or imported records
Attach systems, units, media, documents, and lifecycle facts
Validate record completeness and flag missing core context
Expose the record to request, case, and reporting workflows

workflowProposals.name: Role and delegated authority resolution
workflowProposals.description: Determines who can view, request, approve, coordinate, or receive updates for a property or community item.
steps: Capture owners, residents, tenants, board members, managers, and delegates
Map authority by property, unit, association, and workflow type
Apply approval and visibility rules during actions
Log changes to authority relationships over time

workflowProposals.name: Multi-property rollup and control management
workflowProposals.description: Aggregates records, workload, and performance across multiple properties or communities under one operator view.
steps: Group properties or associations into a portfolio
Roll up open cases, recurring work, spend indicators, and documents
Enable portfolio filters and exception monitoring
Support property-level drilldown from aggregate views

workflowProposals.name: AI-assisted request intake and intent structuring
workflowProposals.description: Transforms free-form issue reports into structured operational records with next-step recommendations.
steps: Capture free-form text, photos, files, urgency, and access notes
Interpret intent, category, likely trade, and severity
Request missing details when confidence is low
Create or route the request into the appropriate case or association queue

workflowProposals.name: End-to-end service case orchestration
workflowProposals.description: Manages cases from triage through completion, closure, and post-resolution recordkeeping.
steps: Open a case from intake or proactive work
Advance through triage, coordination, scheduling, execution, and closure states
Enforce required checkpoints such as approvals or evidence capture
Archive outcome details and update related records

workflowProposals.name: Provider discovery, qualification, and matching
workflowProposals.description: Finds and ranks vendors based on fit, trust, history, and operating constraints.
steps: Filter providers by trade, geography, and availability signals
Check credentials such as licensing, insurance, and service suitability
Blend reputation and prior performance data into ranking
Present matched providers or assisted outreach options

workflowProposals.name: External vendor outreach and coordination
workflowProposals.description: Coordinates work with vendors whether or not they are full platform users.
steps: Send job details and availability requests to vendors
Track responses, schedule proposals, and required documents
Capture updates, delays, and evidence through lightweight collaboration channels
Sync vendor communications back into the central case record

workflowProposals.name: Job scheduling, dispatch, and field progress management
workflowProposals.description: Converts approved work into scheduled jobs with technician assignment and live execution tracking.
steps: Offer or select appointment windows
Assign crews or technicians based on capacity and skill
Deliver job packets to mobile workers
Track check-in, in-progress updates, delays, and completion

workflowProposals.name: Estimate, proposal, and change-order governance
workflowProposals.description: Creates, compares, negotiates, and approves scopes of work before execution.
steps: Generate estimate or bid with structured scope and pricing
Route proposal to the authorized approver
Capture revisions, alternates, or change orders
Record final approval decision and release work

workflowProposals.name: Invoice issuance and payment tracking
workflowProposals.description: Handles financial closeout after work completion, including invoice generation, receipt tracking, and status visibility.
steps: Create invoice from completed work and approved scope
Deliver invoice or payment request to the responsible party
Track payment status, receipts, and exceptions
Update case and financial status once settled

workflowProposals.name: Unified operational messaging and notification routing
workflowProposals.description: Centralizes messages, alerts, reminders, and threaded collaboration across stakeholders.
steps: Attach messages to the relevant property, case, or governance item
Route notifications based on role, authority, and urgency
Preserve thread context across email, portal, and internal actions
Reflect major communication milestones in timelines

workflowProposals.name: Operational document and evidence lifecycle
workflowProposals.description: Stores and organizes documents, media, permits, approvals, and completion artifacts for reuse across workflows.
steps: Capture files from intake, field work, governance, or external uploads
Classify artifacts by property, case, association, and document type
Apply role-based access and retention rules
Make evidence retrievable during future decisions and audits

workflowProposals.name: Material decision logging and audit timeline generation
workflowProposals.description: Maintains an authoritative record of who decided what, when, why, and with which supporting evidence.
steps: Capture decision event with actor identity and timestamp
Store rationale, related documents, and impacted records
Expose chronological ledger views by property, case, or community
Support later retrieval for disputes, governance, or historical review

workflowProposals.name: Constraint and compliance checkpoint evaluation
workflowProposals.description: Detects permits, HOA rules, policy constraints, or other conditions that affect whether and how work can proceed.
steps: Evaluate requested work against known property and community constraints
Flag required permits, approvals, or policy exceptions
Block or reroute work that lacks prerequisites
Store resolved compliance outcomes in the case record

workflowProposals.name: Association work-item and community operations management
workflowProposals.description: Tracks association tasks, common-area work, owner interactions, and operational follow-through.
steps: Create an association work item or owner matter
Assign responsibility and target resolution path
Coordinate vendors, owners, and staff around the item
Close the item with final status and supporting record

workflowProposals.name: Board review, meeting, and approval workflow
workflowProposals.description: Supports governance actions such as agenda preparation, board review, voting, and archival of meeting decisions.
steps: Prepare agenda items with linked documents and operational context
Route materials to board members securely
Record comments, votes, approvals, or deferrals
Publish outcomes to the appropriate downstream operations

workflowProposals.name: Role-based portal task delivery
workflowProposals.description: Serves tailored self-service tasks, records, and updates to homeowners, residents, vendors, managers, and boards.
steps: Authenticate the user and resolve role-based entitlements
Present relevant tasks, records, and status views
Collect actions such as request submission, confirmations, or approvals
Sync portal actions back to core workflows

workflowProposals.name: Recurring maintenance planning and renewal
workflowProposals.description: Schedules and manages preventive maintenance, inspections, and seasonal service cycles across homes, portfolios, and communities.
steps: Define recurring service templates and cadences
Generate upcoming work items based on schedule or season
Assign vendors or providers using historical preferences
Track completion and roll forward the next service cycle

workflowProposals.name: Trade-specific job schema and artifact handling
workflowProposals.description: Applies trade-aware intake, scope structures, and execution artifacts instead of forcing a generic job model.
steps: Select or infer the relevant trade specialization
Load trade-specific questions, scope templates, and required artifacts
Adapt estimates, field checklists, and evidence requirements to the trade
Feed trade-aware outputs into the common case and billing lifecycle

workflowProposals.name: Operational KPI aggregation and exception reporting
workflowProposals.description: Calculates dashboards and performance views across properties, providers, portfolios, and communities.
steps: Aggregate case, timing, vendor, and workload data across domains
Compute KPIs such as response time, completion time, repeat issue rate, and approval lag
Surface exceptions and trend changes to the relevant operator
Support drilldown from summary metrics to underlying records

workflowProposals.name: Internal operations supervision and trust administration
workflowProposals.description: Supports Hestami staff in managing assisted cases, workflow quality, user administration, and AI-human operational controls.
steps: Queue cases requiring staff intervention or exception handling
Provide tools for user administration, reassignment, and quality review
Track AI suggestions, human overrides, and service-level adherence
Escalate risks or anomalies to internal operators

integrationProposals.name: Identity and Single Sign-On
integrationProposals.description: User authentication, MFA, passwordless login, enterprise SSO, and lifecycle controls for homeowners, vendors, managers, board members, and internal staff.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-2, WF-16, WF-17, and WF-21 by enforcing secure role-based access across portals, board workflows, and internal administration; delegated ownership is appropriate because identity assurance, MFA, and federation are better handled by specialized providers.

integrationProposals.name: Payment Gateway and Card Processing
integrationProposals.description: Online payment acceptance, settlement, refunds, payment intents, and receipt confirmation for invoices, deposits, and homeowner or association payments.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-10 and UJ-1, UJ-6, UJ-8 by enabling invoice payment and status tracking without building card processing or PCI-heavy flows in-house.

integrationProposals.name: ACH and Bank Transfer Payments
integrationProposals.description: Bank-account-based payment rails for larger invoices, association payments, and lower-fee settlement options.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-10 and UJ-3, UJ-8, UJ-10 where higher-value repair, reserve, or community payments often require ACH instead of cards; delegated ownership avoids direct money movement complexity.

integrationProposals.name: Accounting and ERP Sync
integrationProposals.description: Synchronization of invoices, payments, vendors, chart-of-accounts mappings, and financial status with external accounting systems.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-10, WF-15, and WF-20 for service providers, landlords, and association operators who need billing records reflected in their financial systems; synced ownership matches bidirectional financial reconciliation needs.

integrationProposals.name: Bank Feed and Reconciliation Sync
integrationProposals.description: Pulls bank transaction data and links settlements or outbound payments to invoices and operational records for reconciliation visibility.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-10 and WF-20 for payment verification and financial transparency in portfolio and association contexts; consumed ownership fits one-way ingestion of bank data into platform workflows.

integrationProposals.name: Email and SMS Delivery
integrationProposals.description: Transactional email and text delivery for notifications, reminders, estimate approvals, appointment updates, and vendor outreach.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-7, WF-8, WF-11, and WF-17 across resident updates, vendor coordination, and portal notifications; delegated ownership is appropriate for deliverability, throughput, and carrier compliance.

integrationProposals.name: Voice Calling and Call Logging
integrationProposals.description: Programmatic calling, masked numbers, voicemail capture, and call event logging for assisted coordination and urgent outreach.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-7, WF-11, and WF-21 plus UJ-5 where Hestami staff need accountable human coordination with vendors or residents; delegated ownership reduces telecom complexity.

integrationProposals.name: Calendar and Availability Sync
integrationProposals.description: Two-way calendar integration for technician availability, appointment booking, reminders, and reschedule coordination.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-8, WF-18, UJ-6, and UJ-7 by aligning job visits and recurring service occurrences with real provider calendars; synced ownership is needed for booking and updates in both systems.

integrationProposals.name: Maps, Geocoding, and Travel Time
integrationProposals.description: Address validation, geocoding, routing, drive-time estimation, and service-area calculations for properties and dispatch operations.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-1, WF-6, and WF-8 by validating property locations, matching nearby providers, and optimizing technician dispatch; delegated ownership is appropriate because map data is external by nature.

integrationProposals.name: Property Data Enrichment
integrationProposals.description: External parcel, assessor, deed, building characteristic, and transaction history enrichment for canonical property records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-1, WF-3, and UJ-1, UJ-3 by accelerating creation and enrichment of property records with trusted third-party facts; consumed ownership fits one-way import into Hestami as the canonical operational system.

integrationProposals.name: Document and Media Object Storage
integrationProposals.description: Durable file storage, versioned media retention, presigned upload flows, and lifecycle management for photos, permits, and evidence bundles.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-12, WF-13, WF-16, and WF-19 where large files, photos, videos, and archival artifacts must be stored securely and durably; delegated ownership is appropriate for scalable storage primitives.

integrationProposals.name: E-Signature and Digital Acceptance
integrationProposals.description: Signature capture and audit-backed acceptance for estimates, approvals, board packets, delegated authority documents, and change orders.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-2, WF-9, and WF-16 by formalizing approvals and authority grants with tamper-evident acceptance records; delegated ownership reduces legal and evidentiary implementation risk.

integrationProposals.name: License and Credential Verification
integrationProposals.description: Verification of contractor licenses, business registrations, and credential status against jurisdictional or commercial registries.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6, WF-14, and UJ-1, UJ-8 by validating provider trust signals before matching or approval; consumed ownership fits pull-based verification against external registries.

integrationProposals.name: Insurance and Certificate Verification
integrationProposals.description: Collection and validation of certificates of insurance, policy status, and coverage evidence for vendors and trades.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6, WF-7, and WF-14 by ensuring vendors remain insurable and compliant for residential and association work; delegated ownership is appropriate because certificate tracking is specialized and externally sourced.

integrationProposals.name: Permit and Jurisdiction Data Access
integrationProposals.description: Lookup of permit requirements, municipal jurisdiction details, and externally published application status or permit records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-14 and WF-19 by identifying whether planned work needs municipal approval and by informing permit applications or compliance evaluations before scheduling.

integrationProposals.name: HOA and Governing Document Import
integrationProposals.description: Import and parsing of CCRs, bylaws, architectural standards, reserve studies, and policy documents from external sources or prior management systems.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-14, WF-15, and WF-16 by bringing existing association rules and governance records into the compliance and board decision workflows; consumed ownership fits migration and read-heavy reference use cases.

integrationProposals.name: CRM and Lead Sync
integrationProposals.description: Synchronization of leads, customer accounts, opportunities, and vendor sales pipeline context with external CRM systems.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6, WF-7, and WF-21 for provider acquisition, assisted case management, and internal operations where external GTM systems may remain source-of-truth for contact and opportunity data.

integrationProposals.name: AI Language and Extraction Services
integrationProposals.description: Large language model and multimodal inference for request interpretation, summarization, routing suggestions, document extraction, and handoff support.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-4, WF-11, WF-12, WF-19, and WF-21 by powering AI-assisted intake, summarization, extraction, and human-AI handoffs; delegated ownership fits rapid model improvement and multimodal capabilities.

integrationProposals.name: OCR and Structured Document Extraction
integrationProposals.description: Optical character recognition and document field extraction for invoices, permits, meeting packets, insurance certificates, and scanned records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-12, WF-14, and WF-16 by converting uploaded operational documents into searchable, structured artifacts that can drive compliance and governance workflows.

integrationProposals.name: Analytics Warehouse and BI Export
integrationProposals.description: Pipeline export of operational events and facts into analytical storage and business intelligence tooling for KPI computation and portfolio reporting.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-20 and UJ-3, UJ-10 by enabling cross-property and cross-community performance analysis at scale; synced ownership reflects ongoing movement of curated operational data into analytics systems.

integrationProposals.name: Observability and Telemetry
integrationProposals.description: Centralized logs, traces, metrics, alerting, and incident telemetry for product reliability, auditability, and internal quality operations.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-20 and WF-21 by giving Hestami staff and engineers visibility into workflow latency, failed notifications, AI handoffs, and system health; delegated ownership is appropriate for mature telemetry pipelines.

integrationProposals.name: Mobile Push Notification Delivery
integrationProposals.description: Push delivery to mobile apps for dispatch changes, board review prompts, tenant updates, and internal escalation alerts.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-8, WF-11, WF-17, and WF-21 where time-sensitive updates must reach field staff, residents, or approvers immediately; delegated ownership fits platform-specific push infrastructure.

integrationProposals.name: Web Conferencing and Virtual Meetings
integrationProposals.description: Virtual meeting creation, joining, and archival links for board meetings, vendor reviews, and internal assisted service coordination.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-16 and WF-21 by enabling remote governance and staffed coordination workflows without building conferencing in-house.

integrationProposals.name: Business and Background Screening
integrationProposals.description: Screening services for provider trust, fraud reduction, and risk signals on organizations or individuals participating in sensitive workflows.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6 and WF-21 where Hestami may need stronger trust controls before surfacing providers broadly or granting operational access; delegated ownership fits regulated verification workflows.

integrationProposals.name: External Review and Reputation Feeds
integrationProposals.description: Import of provider ratings, review summaries, and business profile signals from external reputation platforms when permitted.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6 and UJ-1 by augmenting provider matching with external trust indicators beyond internal performance history; consumed ownership matches one-way signal ingestion.

qualityAttributes: All tenant-scoped reads and writes must enforce server-side tenant isolation, and cross-tenant access attempts must be blocked and auditable.
All sensitive data at rest, including documents, media, messages, and payment-related records, must be encrypted using managed keys with annual key rotation.
All browser, mobile, and API traffic must use TLS 1.2 or higher, and plaintext transport must be rejected.
Every material workflow action affecting approvals, payments, decisions, authority grants, compliance evaluations, or access control must emit an immutable audit event within 5 seconds.
Role-based access checks for property, community, portfolio, and case objects must be evaluated on every request and must not rely solely on client-side filtering.
External vendor access links must be single-purpose, time-bounded, revocable, and expire by default within 14 days.
The platform must support MFA for internal admins, board members, and management-company users before production launch.
P95 API response time for interactive portal actions must remain under 800 ms under the target production load.
Notification delivery status for email, SMS, and push messages must be recorded per endpoint with retry outcomes and final disposition.
The system must achieve 99.9 percent monthly uptime for core portal, intake, case, and messaging workflows, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
Uploads of documents, images, and videos must be virus-scanned before they become available for download or workflow use.
All user-visible timestamps must preserve original event time and timezone context and render correctly for each user locale.
Search across properties, cases, documents, and conversations must return results from authorized scopes only and must reflect newly committed data within 60 seconds.
The system must preserve complete version history for document records, approval artifacts, and governance materials, with rollback visibility but no silent overwrite.
Board votes, approval decisions, and decision-ledger entries must be tamper-evident and must retain actor identity, timestamp, rationale, and evidence links.
The platform must provide point-in-time database recovery with a recovery point objective of 15 minutes and a recovery time objective of 4 hours.
All AI-generated classifications, summaries, or recommendations used in operational workflows must store model version, prompt context reference, confidence, and human override outcome.

PII export, deletion, and retention operations must support policy-based enforcement by record type, including separate retention rules for audit trails that cannot be hard-deleted by standard admins.
Accessibility for portal and internal web experiences must meet WCAG 2.1 AA for keyboard navigation, color contrast, focus visibility, and screen-reader labeling.
Scheduling and dispatch workflows must prevent double-booking of the same technician or crew for overlapping confirmed job visits.
Payment records must be idempotent so duplicate callbacks or retries cannot create duplicate receipts or overstate invoice settlement.
Compliance evaluations must be recomputed whenever scope, trade, property jurisdiction, or governing rule inputs change, and stale evaluations must be visibly marked.
Observability coverage must include structured logs, distributed traces, and service-level metrics for intake, matching, messaging, billing, and authentication paths.
Any production change to workflow configuration, portal visibility rules, or approval policy must be attributable to a named actor and recoverable through configuration history.
Bulk portfolio and community dashboards must calculate KPI observations from a reproducible metric definition and display data freshness timestamps on every view.
uxRequirements: The experience should minimize user effort for both property owners and service providers, consistent with the stated mission.
Property context, service context, and decision history should remain visible and understandable throughout a workflow.
Users should be able to act from rich digital property assets such as photos, videos, documents, and historical records.
The product should support role-appropriate experiences for owners, residents, service providers, managers, boards, and Hestami staff.
Comparisons and recommendations should increase trust by making provider options, rationale, and supporting evidence understandable.
Portal and operational workflows should reduce manual follow-up by exposing status and next actions clearly.
The product should feel like an integrated system rather than disconnected point tools across the three pillars.

requirements.text: The product must support three pillars: homeowner assistance, service provider field service management, and community association management.
The homeowner pillar must allow users to add or update a property with address and uploaded media such as photos and videos.
The homeowner pillar must allow homeowners to create a service call case.
The product must support service provider discovery and matching facilitated by AI agents or Hestami staff.
The homeowner pillar must support property and ownership modeling, including ownership roles, delegated authority, and relationships.
The homeowner pillar must support property portfolio management for owners managing multiple properties.
The homeowner pillar must support free-form owner intent and request intake.
The homeowner pillar must support a durable service call case lifecycle from intake through resolution.
The homeowner pillar must manage property documents, permits, approvals, and correspondence as assistive inputs.
The homeowner pillar must support human Hestami staff service-call execution and outcome recording.
The homeowner pillar must track HOA constraints and approvals even when the HOA is not a platform participant.
The homeowner pillar must support external vendor coordination without requiring vendor onboarding.
The homeowner pillar must maintain a transparent ledger of material decisions, rationale, and supporting evidence.
The CAM pillar must cover association and property operations including work orders, violations, ARC requests, governance, communications, owner portal, documents, and reserve studies.
The CAM pillar must provide role-based portal experiences for homeowners, residents, managers, vendors, and board members.
The service-provider pillar must provide comprehensive field service management for contractors across job lifecycle, scheduling, dispatch, estimating, invoicing, and recurring service operations.
The service-provider pillar must support targeted trade industries rather than a generic business-management use case.

decisions.text: Hestami is being positioned as an integrated end-to-end real property operating system rather than a single-purpose point solution.
The product is structured around three pillars: homeowner assistance, service-provider operations, and community association management.
The initial target customers for the homeowner pillar are individual homeowners, small landlords, and property investors managing one or more residences.
The homeowner pillar is intended to reduce friction in matching property owners and service providers through digital asset exchange and rich property context.
Human-assisted operations are part of the product model; Hestami staff are expected to participate directly in service-call execution rather than the platform being purely self-serve.
The homeowner pillar should preserve transparent decision and rationale history as a trust feature, not just transactional workflow state.
The product intends to support external vendor coordination even when vendors are not formally onboarded onto the platform.
The service-provider pillar is being shaped with ServiceTitan as a reference product, implying a comprehensive contractor operating system for trades rather than a lightweight lead-referral tool.
The CAM pillar is being shaped with Vantaca as a reference product, implying an all-in-one community management system of action for managers, boards, and residents.

constraints.text: The product scope is broad and spans three major business pillars, which creates a sequencing constraint on what can realistically ship first.
Phase 1 must deliver meaningful value for property owners before the broader provider and CAM platform ambitions are realized.
The homeowner experience must work even when the HOA is not a participant in the platform.
The homeowner experience must also work when service providers are coordinated externally rather than fully onboarded into Hestami.
Potential external partner integrations are expected in the long term but explicitly out of scope at this time.
The platform vision assumes long-lived property and governance history, which constrains the product toward persistent record-keeping and continuity across changing participants.

openQuestions.text: What is the specific initial user wedge inside Phase 1: owner-occupied homeowners, small landlords, or multi-property investors?
What is the intended commercial model for each pillar: subscription, transaction fee, managed-service fee, marketplace take rate, or a combination?
How much of the homeowner journey at launch should be AI-self-serve versus Hestami-staff-operated coordination?
What outcome defines success for Phase 1 beyond complete implementation, such as matched jobs, completed jobs, retained users, or revenue?
Which property-service categories should be supported first in the homeowner pillar: repairs, maintenance, improvements, emergency services, or a narrower subset?
Should Phase 1 include homeowner-facing bid comparison and scheduling experiences directly, or only case creation and provider matching?
What level of provider curation is expected in early phases: open internet discovery, vetted network, invite-only providers, or mixed mode?
Which trade industries should be prioritized first in the service-provider pillar when it expands beyond homeowner coordination?
How tightly integrated must the three pillars be at launch versus operating as staged products that share a common long-term vision?
For the CAM pillar, is the target customer independent associations, community management companies, self-managed boards, or all three?
What business capability from ServiceTitan is essential to emulate in Hestami Phase 2, and what should explicitly remain out of scope?
What business capability from Vantaca is essential to emulate in Hestami Phase 3, and what should explicitly remain out of scope?
What exact role should residents or tenants play in the homeowner and CAM experiences relative to owners?
What product boundaries separate Hestami from becoming a full managed service business versus a software platform with assisted workflows?

openLoops.description: Decide the initial Phase 1 market wedge among owner-occupied homeowners, small landlords, or multi-property investors so scope, onboarding, and launch journeys stay coherent.
Define the commercial model by pillar, including whether revenue comes from subscription, transaction fees, managed-service fees, marketplace take rate, or a blended model.
Set the launch operating model for homeowner coordination, especially the boundary between AI self-serve automation and Hestami staff-assisted operations.
Choose the concrete Phase 1 success metrics and target outcomes, such as matched jobs, completed jobs, user retention, conversion, or revenue milestones.
Narrow the initial service-category scope and determine whether Phase 1 includes direct bid comparison and scheduling or stops at structured case creation and provider matching.
Decide the early provider-network model, including the level of curation, vetting, invite-only participation, open discovery, or mixed-mode sourcing.
Clarify how tightly the three pillars must interoperate at launch versus being staged products that share a long-term architecture and data model.
Define the first expansion priorities for the provider pillar, including which trade categories are supported first and which ServiceTitan-like capabilities are in or out of scope for Phase 2.

Define the target customer and scope boundary for the CAM pillar, including whether the Phase 3 focus is independent associations, management companies, self-managed boards, or a subset, and which Vantaca-like capabilities are essential versus excluded.
Clarify the role of residents or tenants relative to owners across homeowner and association experiences, especially for self-service, approvals, communications, and visibility boundaries.
Set explicit product boundaries between software platform behavior and full managed-service operations so the launch model does not drift into labor-intensive service delivery without intent.
